Okay, so welcome to section 8. You are officially beyond the midway point of this program. So it is time to get a little personal. So now that we've dispelled some myths, slaughtered some sacred cows, and filled that vacuum with the new empowering beliefs that your product and your product alone can satisfy, the question then becomes, why the hell should I believe you? And it's a very fair question. You can't assume that everyone who lands on your sales page is super warm, trusts you, and is familiar enough with you, and they've been following you since the beginning. You actually need to assume the worst. You need to assume that they know very little about you, and have no reason to trust you more than that drunken street prophet preaching about the end of times. So that's kind of the mindset you want to go into this with. You want to give them a reason to believe. And you actually do have an advantage here. Your prospects want to believe you. They may be skeptical, but if they resonated with the moment of highest pleasure and the moment of highest tension, then they already have a vested interest in believing you. And if you've done those two sections well, then the battle is already really half won. But here's where you need to take it to the next level and really start making the source of that trust more specific to the solution you're about to offer, as well as their ability to receive the benefit of it. So the first step in doing that will be your bio. And here's what this section will be coaching. So at a very high level, you are coaching trust in your ability to deliver results. And you start this by delivering trust through mentioning past experiences, past clients, and past wins as it relates to the big promise, as well as by giving a quick glimpse into your personal life taste habits. One of the greatest and simplest marketing lessons I've ever learned was from a film director actually named Ali Selim, whose director is selling like 850 television commercials, directed TV shows like Criminal Minds and The Looming Tower. I think he even won like a Golden Lion Award at CONS, which is pretty much like the Oscar for creative marketing. And this guy told me when he was reviewing one of my screenplays that the reader doesn't need to like you. They just need to know that you are like them. And that's why we use a lot of these personal moment seeds, aspects of your personal life that may or may not be likable on the surface, but in a flash and an instant, your prospect realizes you are real. Now, this is important. You do want to inject those, but you don't want to go overboard. You want to keep it short and not self-indulgent. I like to do a mini Kardashian test. You know, if it feels like it's getting self-indulgent and you're just kind of like airing all your dirty laundry on there, you're probably going a little overboard. But just by being open and offering that glimpse and making them realize you are real and there's aspects of your life that they can really resonate around and say they are like me. That's what you're going for here. So yeah, that's what we're doing. We're generating trust also through your method and your process. You're going to mention your IP here. You're going to mention that you're the creator of some sort of unique way of doing things. And finally, you're going to generate trust through taking a stand against common enemies. You really want your prospect to know that you are against the same things that haven't worked for them or the same things that really rub them the wrong way. So you're going to create that unique bonding point between them. And through all these elements, you're going to generate a lot of trust in just a few short paragraphs. So where are you going to get this information from? I do recommend you go back to sections three and four of the 67 questions workbook. Look at that ethos graphic data, especially as that's really where you're going to get a lot of those bonding points and points of inspiration for you to include in that whole. They need to know I am like them component of the testimonial. So let's see how this plays out in real time. We've got this one by Greg Hickman again. So who the heck am I to tell you this? What's up Greg Hickman here? And when I'm not serenading my beautiful wife Sarah, son Cole and dog Koda with the best of old school nineties hip hop, I run system dot lean, a funnel strategy and automation agency that has worked behind the scenes engineering six and seven figure campaigns for some of the rock stars you look up to people like John Lee Dumas, Chris Stucker, Jasmine star, Betty rocker, etc. So we get all that personal glimpse into his life, specific names, even the names of his dog to really bring it to life. And I do encourage you to use specificity. And here we're getting into taking a stand against those enemies. We are not info marketers, funnel hackers overpriced. Let me tell you what to do, but not how to do it business consultants. And then the USP we are hardcore implementation specialists and next level funnel strategists that pick you up on the side of the thorny back roads that those other folks left you off at. And if you allow me to do it, here's where I plan to taking, here's where I plan on taking you and your business. And we have another one. So this is the one I used for 10 X launches last year. Meet the original conversion code. So right away injecting that IP that method that process more reclusive than Salander on a rainy day. Raj was has been deep in the trenches for over a dozen six and seven figure product launches and effort green funnels. When he's not sipping single malts writing screenplays or trying to understand why people keep losing their shit over stranger things. He's an he's on he's on airplane mode writing or consulting with top online entrepreneurs, experts and educators, like Amy Porterfield, Todd Herman, Josh up in Dan Martell. So right there we have those points of recognition, like I don't get stranger things. I drink single malts and all these things that writers tend to understand and relate with. And he's done it all without writing a single line of copy. Instead, he developed the coaching the conversion method as a faster, more natural and intuitive framework for crafting launch copy that connects deeply with your prospects and coaches them into being the perfect buyer. So generating trust through process and IP once again, meaning you get to enjoy more profitable value driven relationships with your clients instead of feeling like a greased up secondhand sham wow dealer every time you hit sense. So taking a stand against something that our readers would never feel good about. So those are the two examples we have. I encourage you to review those ethos graphic points for those points of relatability if you don't have them yet. Otherwise get creative, have fun with this. Don't be a Kardashian and enjoy.
